title: Prove the real spawn path once: worktree, runtime pane, live agent, then clean up
action: approach.md requires one real split into a real worktree; until this runs, the code path that creates a worktree and starts an unattended editing agent has zero evidence behind it. Execute the dispatch action ONCE for real, using the literal throwaway slug `apo-throwaway-spawn` and nothing else: `bee worktree new --feature apo-throwaway-spawn`, `herdr pane split --cwd <that worktree>` on the runtime tab, `herdr agent start` with argv carrying `--model sonnet --permission-mode bypassPermissions`. Confirm the new pane self-names to the worktree name (D17) and that `herdr pane list` shows it in the runtime tab with a live session. Record the observed pane id, the observed label, the exact argv, and the pane count before and after - observed values, never a description of them. Then tear it all down: close the pane, `git worktree remove`, delete the `wt/apo-throwaway-spawn` branch, drop the grant, and show the pane count and grant set back at their starting values. This is the one place a human sees the unattended spawn before it is ever left running.

truths: A real worktree named apo-throwaway-spawn was created and a real runtime pane opened with it as cwd
The pane self-named to the worktree name with nothing else labelling it
The recorded argv contains --model sonnet and --permission-mode bypassPermissions
After teardown no grant, no worktree, and no branch named apo-throwaway-spawn remains
The recorded before and after pane counts are equal
The document records observed pane id, label, argv and counts, not descriptions of them

artifacts.substantive: observed evidence of one real spawn and its clean teardown

prohibitions: Do not leave the throwaway worktree, branch, pane or grant behind
Do not use any slug other than apo-throwaway-spawn
Do not run bee worktree merge; merging is slice 2

verify: set -e; F=.claude/skills/herdr-orchestrating/references/spawn-proof.md; test -s "$F"; grep -q 'bypassPermissions' "$F"; grep -q -- '--model sonnet' "$F"; grep -qE '\bw[0-9A-Za-z]+:p[0-9A-Za-z]+\b' "$F"; grep -q 'apo-throwaway-spawn' "$F"; node .bee/bin/bee.mjs worktree list --json | node -e "let s='';process.stdin.on('data',d=>s+=d).on('end',()=>{if(Object.keys(JSON.parse(s).grants||{}).some(k=>k.includes('apo-throwaway-spawn'))){console.error('grant not dropped');process.exit(1)}})"; test -z "$(git worktree list | grep 'apo-throwaway-spawn' || true)"; test -z "$(git branch --list 'wt/apo-throwaway-spawn')"; diff -q "$F" .agents/skills/herdr-orchestrating/references/spawn-proof.md; echo SPAWN_PROVEN_AND_CLEAN

trace.outcome: One real spawn observed end to end and fully torn down, which exposed two wiring facts no dry run could have: agent start opens its own pane, and a headless argv closes the pane before anything can be seen.
trace.verify_command: set -e; F=.claude/skills/herdr-orchestrating/references/spawn-proof.md; test -s "$F"; grep -q 'bypassPermissions' "$F"; grep -q -- '--model sonnet' "$F"; grep -qE '\bw[0-9A-Za-z]+:p[0-9A-Za-z]+\b' "$F"; grep -q 'apo-throwaway-spawn' "$F"; node .bee/bin/bee.mjs worktree list --json | node -e "let s='';process.stdin.on('data',d=>s+=d).on('end',()=>{if(Object.keys(JSON.parse(s).grants||{}).some(k=>k.includes('apo-throwaway-spawn'))){console.error('grant not dropped');process.exit(1)}})"; test -z "$(git worktree list | grep 'apo-throwaway-spawn' || true)"; test -z "$(git branch --list 'wt/apo-throwaway-spawn')"; diff -q "$F" .agents/skills/herdr-orchestrating/references/spawn-proof.md; echo SPAWN_PROVEN_AND_CLEAN
